Hey everyone, Duran here over at Frank Kern's team here to show you how to set up Google Analytics as well as Google Tag Manager in order to prep so we can install the Facebook Cappy which is conversions API. So let's get right into it. The first thing we need to do is we need to go to analytics.google.com and I'm going to try my best to act as if I was starting it from the beginning. So the first thing you want to do is you want to go to admin and you want to go to create property. Once you're going to create the property we'll do GA tutorial v2 and we will keep it as United States, Los Angeles and then you want to click show advanced options and you want to click this create a universal analytics property and you want to make sure it says create both analytics for and universal analytics property. Now the difference between these two is that the GA4 is something that is pretty much new and it's able to not just track page views but also anything that may be embeddable such as Calendly, a form, whatever it may be, it could still track it while the universal analytics property was what Google Analytics started with from the get go and this one only tracks page views. So we want to have both and we're going to put the website, we'll just do frankkern.com and we're going to click next and you can put in whatever you want here. I'm going to do beauty and fitness and I would just select everything. Think doesn't really matter and then click create and boom. Now we have a new container and this property here is you could have more than just one property so let me explain. You could have a container and a property just for your website and then you could have a container and property for another website that could be a parent website or a child website. You could also have a container for your app. If you want to be able to narrow down and filter out different traffic sources, you could do that by creating different containers and adding that Google site tag which we're not going to do right away because we're technically going to install Google Tag Manager and Google Tag Manager is going to have this code within it. So we're going to keep this on the side for now and what we're going to do is we're going to go to Google Tag Manager and it's very easy. It's free to sign up and what I'm going to do is I'm going to create a new account which I did. I created a new container name. Now what you do is you click into the container and then what you want to do is you want to go to tags and you want to create new. Now we're going to go to tag configuration and you're going to want to set up Google Analytics, Universal Analytics first. We're actually going to do both. All right. So give me one second and I will give you an explanation on how to do this. Give me one second. All right. So there's one thing I wanted to show you because I want you to see how to set it up. So I'm actually going to go back and I'm going to create a new account and we're going to call this GTM tutorial and we're going to put the site www.francern.com and we're going to select web for now. Okay. Then we're going to click create. We're going to accept. And now what's going to happen is you're going to have these codes right here, which we're going to have to install. So I'm going to go ahead and go into frankern.com and I will install this or it could be your website obviously and you want to install this one in your header and this one in the body and this could be click funnels. This could be unbounce. It could be WordPress basically anywhere where traffic is coming to a page that you own. You want to make sure that these tracking codes are in there. So let me pause for one second and load up these tags here for you. All right. So I logged into Frank Kerns for the WordPress. This will be a WordPress example. And now you need to add it to your header and footer. Now mind you, a lot of themes have their own place to put a universal header and footer code. Some don't. So what I suggest you to do is download a plugin called headers that install headers and footers. And I already have it installed. I'm going to put it right here. And we are going to add those codes. So first one copy goes here. Next one goes to the body. And then we're going to click save. Once that's in, we could X out of this. You could always go back and find these. It's not like you need to save it. And once that is done, what you need to do. So what you want to do is go back to analytics and you want to make sure you select, you're going to have two versions. You're going to have GA tutorial V2GA4 and GA tutorial V2UA. You want to make sure you select the UA and then go to property settings and take this tracking ID. Then you're going to go to Google tag manager. You're going to create a new tag. You're going to click here and you're going to select Google analytics, universal analytics. And what you're going to do is you're going to keep this the same page view. All right. You're not going to touch anything over here. And then you're going to add right here the triggering page view. And now there's one more thing you need to do. First of all, let's name this Google analytics. Okay. Then we want to go back here and we want to set up a new variable and you're going to do Google analytics and we're going to do the tracking ID. We're going to copy and paste from where we found it here. All right. And then you could click save. We're going to call this GA prod. Okay. And then we just click save. Make sure it says all page. We're going to click save. And most importantly, make sure you click submit because just because you click save and everything has been done, you need to make sure that it's been published. So you could put a name of the description. It's just telling you when it's been updated. So new Google analytics, GTM, right? Whatever you want it to be. And there we go. That has been set up. Now, now that we have that set up, the next thing you want to do is you want to test it, right? So we're going to go to the website. What you can normally do is just go to real time and then go to overview and then just go to the page, right? That you have a connected to frank.com. And you could see here that currently we have one visitor right now, which is me, right? And you could see that it's working, which is great, right? Good to connect to products. And if I go to about should be pushing to about in a sec. Hopefully. There you go. Right? And that is how you set up Google analytics as well as Google tag manager, because what you want to do is you want to do all your edits in Google tag manager and keep Google analytics isolated by itself. This way you don't need to hire a developer to always make changes whenever you want to add something. Instead, you could use the tags within Google tag manager to create events and fire off events without having to go into Google analytics, which is awesome. Right? The next thing that we're going to go over, which is going to be a separate video will be for the Facebook.
